title: diario
text: R package: thin wrapper around diariodeobras.net (“Diário de Obras”) construction-log platform. stores API token securely performs authenticated requests, returning projects, tasks, reports, etc. Published CRAN. Maintainer: André Leite. Sibling: Python port lives ../diariopy (PyPI diariopy, repo StrategicProjects/diariopy). Keep behaviour parity changing either. READMEs cross-link. Current status: v0.1.2 live CRAN (accepted 2026-06-26). Next dev cycle starts 0.1.2.

title: diario
text: R/diario.R — entire implementation (exported functions live ). tests/testthat/ — test-validation.R (argument checks) test-request.R (request layer, network mocked). man/ — roxygen-generated .Rd (edit hand). _pkgdown.yml + pkgdown/ — site config; built site committed (see “Website” ). NEWS.md, cran-comments.md — keep updated every release.

title: diario
text: user-facing output goes cli (cli_abort, cli_warn, cli_inform, cli_alert_success, cli_alert_warning) — never base stop(), warning(), message(), cat(), print(). Token storage: keyring, service "DiarioAPI_Token", username "global". Base URL overridable via getOption("diario.base_url", ...) (default https://apiexterna.diariodeobra.app/) — used mock tests. Auth header token, Authorization. Argument validators must reject NA (note nzchar(NA) TRUE, always include explicit .na() check alongside nzchar()). Depends R >= 4.1, avoid base---4.4 features like %||%. API Portuguese: response column names many data values come back Portuguese even though function/arg names English. endpoints wrap data envelope (e.g. lista-de-tarefas returns counters + cronograma field; diario_get_task_list() unwraps cronograma).

title: diario
text: Tests need network token — test-request.R stubs token testthat::local_mocked_bindings(diario_retrieve_token = ...) mocks HTTP httr2::with_mocked_responses().
code: devtools::load_all() roxygen2::roxygenise()                  # after changing roxygen comments testthat::test_dir("tests/testthat")    # or devtools::test() devtools::check(document = TRUE, args = "--as-cran")   # CRAN gate; aim for 0/0/0 devtools::build_readme()                # README.md is generated from README.Rmd

title: diario

what: R Interface to the Diariodeobras Application
title: R Interface to the Diariodeobras Application
text: diario provides set functions securely storing API tokens interacting diariodeobras.net system. wraps common functionality (e.g., authentication, GET/POST requests) convenient R functions, making easier integrate Diario within R workflows. 🐍 Using Python? port package available diariopy (PyPI · docs).

title: R Interface to the Diariodeobras Application

title: R Interface to the Diariodeobras Application
text: Store API token: making requests, ’ll need store Diario API token securely. Use diario_store_token(): Retrieve token (debugging confirmation): Make authenticated request: main workhorse diario_perform_request(), sets executes authenticated request Diario API endpoints.
code: library(diario)  # Store your API token securely using keyring diario_store_token("YOUR_API_TOKEN_HERE") diario_retrieve_token() #> "YOUR_API_TOKEN_HERE" # Example: retrieving data from an endpoint response <- diario_perform_request("v1/obras") head(response)

title: Perform an API request to Diario — diario_perform_request
text: endpoint non-empty character string specifying API endpoint. query named list query parameters (optional). method HTTP method use (e.g., "GET", "POST", "PUT", "DELETE"). Default "GET". body list representing JSON body request methods support body (e.g., POST). verbosity Verbosity level request (0 = none, 1 = minimal). Default 0.

title: Perform an API request to Diario — diario_perform_request

title: diario 0.1.2
text: CRAN release: 2026-06-26

title: diario 0.1.2
text: Fixed bug query parameters passed httr2::req_url_query() single list argument, errored (“components ... must named”). now spliced correctly, diario_get_reports()’s limit/order arguments work documented. argument validators now reject missing values (NA). Previously nzchar(NA) returned TRUE, NA token passed straight keyring, NA ids pasted request URLs.

title: diario 0.1.2
text: diario_get_task_list() now returns schedule items (one row per task, _id, descricao, etc.) instead raw API envelope. Previously summary counters recycled across rows actual tasks hidden inside nested cronograma column.

title: diario 0.1.2
text: diario_perform_request() now sends HTTP method upper case, matching validation, lower-case input (e.g. "get") longer leaks . API base URL now configurable via diario.base_url option, making possible target staging environments mock requests tests. HTTP errors now surface message returned Diario API (via httr2::req_error()), empty response bodies (e.g. 204 Content DELETE) handled gracefully instead raising content-type error. diario_get_reports() now validates order one "asc" "desc" front. diario_retrieve_token() gained quiet argument; diario_perform_request() uses avoid emitting duplicate “valid token found” message. Added testthat test suite covering argument validation request layer (network mocked httr2::with_mocked_responses()).

title: diario 0.1.1
text: CRAN release: 2026-04-07

title: diario 0.1.1
text: Migrated user-facing messages, warnings, errors use cli package (cli::cli_abort(), cli::cli_warn(), cli::cli_inform(), cli::cli_alert_success(), cli::cli_alert_warning()), replacing base R stop() message() calls. provides richer, informative output inline markup arguments, values, functions, packages. Fixed bug diario_get_reports() query parameters (limite, ordem) constructed never passed diario_perform_request(). Fixed incorrect example diario_get_projects() documentation referenced query parameter accepted function. Wrapped diario_store_token() diario_retrieve_token() examples \dontrun{} avoid CRAN check failures systems without keyring support. Removed LazyData: true DESCRIPTION (data/ directory exists). Added BugReports field DESCRIPTION. Updated authors list.

title: diario 0.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2025-01-15 Initial CRAN release. Token management via keyring (diario_store_token(), diario_retrieve_token()). Authenticated API requests httr2 (diario_perform_request()). Convenience wrappers: diario_get_company(), diario_get_entities(), diario_get_projects(), diario_get_project_details(), diario_get_task_list(), diario_get_task_details(), diario_get_reports(), diario_get_report_details().
